text:
Vinesh Phogat turned the tide against attempts to discredit wrestlers' protest. | @Phogat_Vinesh/X Vinesh Phogat is a woman of her word. Earlier this year, the Haryana wrestler, discredited after protesting against the sexual harassment-accused former Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) chief Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh, had promised to give her “haters” something to be mad at. Now, after Photogat became the first Indian woman to make the Olympics wrestlingfinal, on the course to which she ended the legendary winning streak—an unbeaten run of 82-0—of Japan’s four-time world champion Yui Susaki, herold X posthas resurfaced. “Dear haters, I have so much for you to be mad at. Just be patient,” the 29 year old said on March 12. 148 days later, Phogat stands on the precipice of history, having ventured where none of her countrywomen have gone before in the 50 kg Freestyle wrestling competition. She has already defeated the gold medalist in an upset for the ages, which Bajrang Punia, who was the face of wrestlers’ protest alongside Sakshi Malik and Phogat herself, described as a “slap on the face of Brij Bhushan”. Taking to X, the three-time Olympianwrote: “Vinesh Phogat is the lioness of India who won back to back matches today. Defeated 4-time World Champion and defending Olympic champion. After that he defeated the former World Champion in the quarterfinals. But let me tell you one thing. This girl was kicked and crushed in her own country. This girl was dragged on the streets in her country. This girl is going to conquer the world but she lost to the system in this country” Many others, who had accused earlier Phogat of protesting for “political reasons” and conspiring with the Indian National Congress (INC) against Brij Bhushan, congratulated her on social media. The wrestler beat Cuba'sYusneylys Guzman Lopez5-0 to earn India's maiden appearance in the final, in which she will take on the USA's Sarah Hildebrandt.

A nature-loving, Pink Floyd-listening, authority-resisting twenty-something somewhere on the spectrum.
title: Old Vinesh Phogat Post Viral After Olympics Win for the Ages: ‘Dear Haters’ | Viral - Times Now
description: “Dear haters, I have so much for you to be mad at. Just be patient,” Vinesh Phogat said in a March 12 post on X. 148 days later, she's on the precipice of history, having become the first Indian to reach the Olympics women's wrestling final, after beating Yui Susaki no less., Viral, Times Now
keywords: vinesh phogat match, vinesh phogat olympics, vinesh phogat yui susaki, vinesh phogat final, vinesh phogat brij bhushan,, Viral, Times Now
